justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Fulfillment, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly identified — e.g., notification context consuming Route Planning data, recalculation triggers spanning Delivery Fulfillment and Route Planning. Vehicle class constraint correctly marked invariant:true (F1), stop duration correctly marked invariant:false (R5/R6), notification window correctly marked invariant:false (N1). 7 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria plus edge case table. All three recalculation triggers enumerated (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation).

observations.text: Four bounded contexts clearly identified with responsibility separation and a well-structured table
Comprehensive ubiquitous language glossary with owning context attribution — exceeds expectations
Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out (e.g., Next-3-Stops Window noted as 'consuming Route Planning data', recalculation triggers spanning contexts)
Invariant classification correct: vehicle class (true), recalculation triggers (true), stop duration (false), notification window (false)
7 acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, well above the 4 minimum
Edge cases table covers recalculation storms, notification retraction, zero-delivery zones — strong defensive analysis
Escalation items explicitly flag ambiguities for product team resolution before implementation — shows domain authority
Cross-context dependency could be more formally structured as integration points for MJ — the dependencies are mentioned but not consolidated into a single 'integration contract' section
Business impact analysis with stakeholder mapping adds strategic depth beyond the rubric requirements

# Domain Analysis — Smart Route Optimization

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This feature encodes the **operational dispatch and route planning process** for a last-mile delivery platform. It sits at the intersection of three core business capabilities: workforce management (driver shifts and licensing), delivery fulfillment (pending deliveries and zone assignment), and customer communication (delivery proximity notifications).
